description:

💛 *If you liked this video, you'll probably like these videos that I mentioned... ⇢*
→ How to Become More Yourself (Prediction Pruning): https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dS_FfkjufAQ&t=66s&pp=0gcJCUELAYcqIYzv
→ The Shame Procrastination Cycle: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZCxuFdIPqJk&t=191s

🍧 *I post more of this kind of thing on Instagram ⇢*
→ Follow me: http://instagram.com/muchelleb

🎧 *Prefer Spotify? ⇢*
→ I'm over there too: https://open.spotify.com/show/2HqznNA2v9McYirvqmUVvO?si=024ac78820b24443

💭 *Video timeline ⇢*

00:00 The Acceptance Paradox
00:29 What is "acceptance"?
01:18 Meredith and the sickness
02:27 The welcome mat mindset
03:04 The goal beneath the goal
04:51 To accept is to observe without judgment
06:19 The Forever Exercise

🔗 *You think this video just fell out of a coconut tree? It exists in the context... ⇢*
🔗 This video title was inspired of course, by the original Carl Rogers quote, but also by the lovely Anna Howard, https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sLsjepPZKd0, alongside the substack article of the same name, https://www.henrikkarlsson.xyz/p/diaries - I made a point not to read or watch either of theseee because I am far too scared of accidental plagiarism, but as soon as I saw that video title I was like oh, I have things to say.
🔗 The story of Meredith was from a book by Brad Stulberg, Master of Change: https://www.amazon.com.au/Master-Change-Everything-Changing-Including-ebook/dp/B0DG48NL6P/?_encoding=UTF8&pd_rd_w=qOJjF&content-id=amzn1.sym.7153b2d3-487c-46f2-9cd8-b060e652f2e8&pf_rd_p=7153b2d3-487c-46f2-9cd8-b060e652f2e8&pf_rd_r=355-7641594-2976438&pd_rd_wg=6K41W&pd_rd_r=06e3a135-9507-431f-9d55-e6b90ee4f7a8
🔗 The altered story of Marjorie was from Lance Dodes work, which I highly recommend: https://www.amazon.com.au/Breaking-Addiction-7-Step-Handbook-Ending-ebook/dp/B004HD61JK/ref=sr_1_1?crid=DXE9HIBJ3N1Q&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.1kxyD7yNzHnY_UJtzGX_6ZoZe9iG5LCEQR9eUHiYL0q2LG8j_oTHTimNqyrpDSFe.99Do7fzTIvS4nGYRjaQC_f_WiwHUEhLu2IYyyzTDbYQ&dib_tag=se&keywords=lance+dodes&qid=1781382211&s=digital-text&sprefix=lance+dod%2Cdigital-text%2C260&sr=1-1
🔗 How To Get Rid Of Headache Or Migraine In 2 Minutes Or Less https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UKGtv84aSjo
🔗 This video is really about radical acceptance, which originated in Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT). It was developed in the late 1980s by psychologist Dr. Marsha Linehan and was also inspired by Buddhism. For a very modern version of radical acceptance, check outttt.. the let them theory haha.
🔗 I really enjoyed this book about acceptance: https://www.amazon.com.au/Acceptance-Create-Change-Move-Forward/dp/1399707930/ref=sr_1_3?crid=2DTURUMQI7EP7&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.BtkrDfBHItOgDVmAD_JMfqBfZzh5rO4_cOM_01m9SnKxWeQF6Y5qQUAdaPBSE0Ar5eD4gtU833uae-4vpdYhK2q34eyYQsuXH6rpb6yZF7xu70d-V8M4hfX8x4wvjAcnCfGWvfwC5nEF0Pp0JBCfDg.bzrdGqKR89HVQCEiwbfAXVUU6TPHY3fDc-oC8nveOyU&dib_tag=se&keywords=acceptance+padraid&qid=1781382531&sprefix=acceptance+padra%2Caps%2C239&sr=8-3
🔗 But if I'm being real, my understanding of acceptance stemmed way more from this book than anything else: https://www.amazon.com.au/Forgive-Good-PROVEN-Prescription-Happiness/dp/006251721X/ref=sr_1_1?crid=1P9033T906N9P&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.2J9j8DKYGSRV_eJ3L6VVu3riheL9Wj1Xh2gQSakePNiFnlNhnhh89EZinqpuZnUy7isBWA-4nkvKSa4Lc7LQhdJc3TGc3M-EZaq4ZoY964S13rNI9DCdbh7T3ojbJuZKNNPpK-OM-7v_eKFfMKgtLQ.l1xlyQ9NmNzBpycQCPwmXp6EQ2lV0MQOHYIFimmUIe0&dib_tag=se&keywords=forgiveness+by+fred+luskin&qid=1781382600&sprefix=forgiveness+by+fred+lusk%2Caps%2C247&sr=8-1

title: How to become *more yourself* (with "prediction pruning")
description:
☁️ *Join the Intention Community! ⇢*
→  _Every month, two brand-new tools (meditations, worksheets, videos, etc) drop, usually built around a recent video - so that you can take what you’ve learned and do something with it._
→ Join: https://intentioncommunity.circle.so/
→ Already a member? Try out The Change Session (it goes hand-in-hand with this video): https://intentioncommunity.circle.so/c/the-change-lab/the-change-session

📝 *Important notes⇢*

🚨 This approach is most suitable for everyday changes (kind of like the examples I talked through in this video), *not* for change related to any events that could be considered catastrophic - please ALWAYS consult a professional if what you're experiencing is related to very difficult events in your life.

🚨 I'm not a mental health professional, just a gal into shame-free, acceptance-based behaviour change.

📝 This isn't the only way to change that requires less "force" and leans more "natural/spontaneous". There are always (at least) 100 ways to climb a mountain! 

📝 For some changes, it also wouldn't make sense to try to apply the ideas in this video. 

📝 For some brains, this approach might not be helpful at all. Always, where you can, try to experiment in small ways to check out what DOES suit you and how YOU operate before diving in and taking any ideas as absolute.

📝 Keep an open mind but also... take what resonates, leave the rest. 

🪞 *Organise your Life with the Calm Productivity Notion Pack. ⇢*

💭 *Video timeline ⇢*
00:00 You are a walking, talking prediction machine
00:52 Your predictions change how you behave
02:44 Buut they aren't conscious
04:43 How to find your predictions
05:53 How to dissolve your predictions
07:42 The goal: Get your brain to recalculate
09:26 Creating a mismatch experience
10:43 How your brain changes through neuroplasticity
12:29 Thank u, next

🔗 *Helpful Links ⇢*
🔗 Book: Redefining Stuttering by John Harrison
*Each stutter may be unique when it comes to cause. This book goes through a number of potential factors that might contribute towards sustaining a long-term stutter from Johns unique perspective. It's a great read. https://www.amazon.com.au/Redefining-Stuttering-struggle-Previously-Speaking/dp/1929773005
🔗 Book: (The book that started it all) Unlocking the Emotional Brain: Memory Reconsolidation and the Psychotherapy of Transformational Change by Bruce Ecker https://www.amazon.com.au/Unlocking-Emotional-Brain-Reconsolidation-Transformational/dp/1032139129
*This book is THE resource to look at if you're interested in how our predictions change our behaviour and shaped a lot of the knowledge shared in this video.
🔗 Book: Little Treatments Big Effects by Jessica Schleider. This book is where Jessica shares her "epiphany" story after trying a hard food. https://www.amazon.com.au/Little-Treatments-Big-Effects-Meaningful/dp/1472147227

title: Why Self-Improvement Can Make You Worse.
description:
💿 *Get my best practical ideas in your inbox (free)⇢*
→ Sign up: https://www.muchelleb.com.au/newsletter-signup

⛳️ Red Flags of Self-Improvement

This is off the top of my head, no specific creators in mind.

A lot of creators who do lean into these lines of thinking often are doing what they feel is helpful, but I'd just be a bit more mindful if this is the style of content you're watching. Really notice how it makes you feel, and, if you use any of the advice, pay attention to whether it leaves you feeling more or less thoughtful. 

→ Encourages isolating yourself (e.g. go ghost for 6 months, cut off everyone, etc.)

→ Over-focuses on “having discipline” and “taking action”, while ignoring emotional regulation, difficult days, different brains, flexibility, and letting go of self-criticism

→ Creates an “us vs them” dynamic that breeds division or superiority (e.g. “people like us vs people like them”)

→ Pushes rigid formulas, steps, or “right ways” of doing things without encouraging self-reflection or a personalised approach

→ Turns relationships into hierarchies (e.g. “high-value vs low-value people”)

→ Encourages self-rejection instead of self-acceptance

→ Feeds your ego or makes you feel “better than” others (e.g. “when you start healing, you won’t relate to people anymore”)

→ Encourages shame-based growth rather than curiosity-led growth

→ Promotes constant optimisation without emphasising space for fun, rest, and relationships

→ Pushes “more, more, more” instead of focusing on what’s actually effective (which is often less)

→ Over-identifies you with your problems in an unhelpful way (leans heavily on labels instead of helping you understand and change what’s actually going on)

title: The shame→procrastination cycle.
description:
💿 *Get my best practical ideas in your inbox (free)⇢*
→ Sign up: https://www.muchelleb.com.au/newsletter-signup

☁️ *Join the Intention Community (it's replaced the Intention app)! ⇢*

→ _"This notion pack is amazing!! I feel like it’s changing my life already and I’ve never been so impressed by a product." Alice Joyce_
→ Get the pack: https://shop.muchelleb.com.au/products/the-calm-productivity-notion-pack

💛 *If you liked this video, you'll probably like... ⇢*
→ The Behaviour Change Playlist _(when you want to start a new habit or change)_ : https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLW3yd6NHmEVqXsWO6qv2AytfUtVeVf6f7

🍧 *I post more of this kind of thing on Instagram ⇢*
→ Follow me: http://instagram.com/muchelleb

🎧 *Prefer chattier deep-dives on productivity and behaviour change? ⇢*
→ I drop weekly chatty episodes on Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/2HqznNA2v9McYirvqmUVvO?si=024ac78820b24443


 💭 Video timeline ⇢

00:00 The Trap That Keeps You Stuck
0:48 Why You Can't Bounce Back
02:01 The Real Reason You’re So Hard on Yourself
03:46 What your Brain Needs to Change
05:37 The 2 Ingredients Required to Actually Break a Pattern
06:47 How to Interrupt the Self-Criticism Loop
07:34 A Thought Experiment to Try
10:40 What to Do Instead of Criticising Yourself
13:18 A Second Way to Break the Cycle

🔗 Recs of The Week⇢  

Want to dive deeper into the topic of today's video? Here are a few resources that you might enjoy... 

🔗 Here is the self-trust playlist that I mentioned with a bunch of my recent podcast episodes on productivity and self-trust: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLjT17RYjxknFkrXITs2Jn5HAD_WNC2bBq

🔗 Firstly, this paper on negative emotions and procrastination is a really interesting read: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12588926/#:~:text=In%20summary%2C%20most%20of%20existing,positive%20correlation%20between%20the%20two.

🔗 Jud Brewer actually wrote a great article on the link between guilt/procrastination loop that I saw after starting to put together this video - great minds - (focused more on the reward of procrastination and body based curiosity) - this article introduced me to the research paper above: https://drjud.com/procrastination/procrastination-guilt/ 

🔗 Jud Brewer also wrote Unwinding Anxiety, which goes in-depth on the idea of "mental" habit loops - he talks specifically about anxiety, but I've found myself applying the concepts in this book to much more: https://amzn.to/4tkVa1e 

🔗 One of the first people I saw talk about the procrastination/guilt cycle is Dr David Maloney, who wrote a great book called Procrastination Decoded that you might enjoy: https://amzn.to/4tkVa1e 

🔗 Michelle Segars book, The Joy Choice, talks about the rewarding experience of curiosity applied to health behaviours and exercise: https://amzn.to/4cGXQRk

🔗 If you're particularly interested in the idea of "prediction errors" triggered by "symptom deprivation" this wonderful book by Bruce Ecker talks in-depth about the process within Coherence Therapy: https://amzn.to/4tkVa1e 

🔗 If the idea of surprise being a key driver of learning is of interest to you, give "How We Learn" a read. I really enjoyed this book: https://amzn.to/4vb4jvh 

🔗  This book on compassion dives into our brain's threat avoidance system and how it can sometimes create efficiency but leave us unhappier: https://amzn.to/3Q40VlD
